title: Document maintainer-account and token hardening expectations
description: Add repo-security guidance covering maintainer account protection, token scope minimization, PAT avoidance where possible, credential rotation expectations, and the risks of privileged automation on a personal repo.

title: Keep dependencies aggressively current, including major versions
description: Adopt an explicit update posture: track current major versions instead of lingering behind, prefer frequent small updates over rare giant jumps, and accept the operational cost as a deliberate hardening choice. This is the top priority in the secure-development backlog.
design: Treat aggressive dependency updates as a safety architecture problem, not just a version-bump chore. This umbrella is not done when a bot opens PRs; it is only mature enough when the repo has checked current-state assumptions, formalized provider/event/deploy contracts, established smoke-gated candidate promotion with automatic rollback, and reached enough automated test maturity to catch dependency breakage before rollout. That means at minimum CI-backed fast checks plus core chat and /hermes smoke coverage are part of the path, not optional follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog is split into assumption-audit, contract, candidate-rollout, sandboxing, docs, and review slices. Repo has a documented systemd-first, rollback-first direction for safe updates. Safe aggressive updates are explicitly gated on some architecture maturity and testing maturity: contracts are documented, smoke-gated rollback exists or is planned through child beads, and CI plus core chat /hermes smoke coverage are tracked as direct prerequisites. Follow-up implementation can proceed from beads and docs without relying on chat history.
notes: This parent bead is the umbrella for safe aggressive updates, not merely dependency automation. Documentation and explicit assumption-checking are mandatory parts of the work, and the bead should not be considered satisfied by updater configuration alone. The awkward parent-child relationship with hermes-tailchat-w9u has been removed; dependency automation now sits as a separate blocked related bead rather than as the parent of the whole architecture track.

title: Audit current architecture assumptions and dependency surfaces for safe aggressive updates
description: Audit the real current architecture, deploy shape, dependency surfaces, runtime authorities, and rollout assumptions behind aggressive dependency updates. This bead must check assumptions rather than inherit stale docs: identify which interfaces exist today, which writable paths and network authorities exist, which package managers/lock surfaces are present, and which invariants candidate deploys must preserve. Ship an updated architecture/current-state note plus an explicit assumptions log that later implementation beads can rely on.
acceptance_criteria: Outputs an updated current-state architecture note, an assumptions log, and a dependency-surface inventory. Explicitly records what was verified versus still provisional. Includes runtime/write-path/network/deploy invariants needed by later beads.

title: Formalize provider, event, and deploy contracts for update-safe changes
description: Define explicit contracts around the dependency seams most likely to break under upgrades: Hermes provider behavior, normalized event/replay semantics, critical frontend rendering behavior, and candidate deploy health criteria. Include test-planning notes that map each contract to evidence, especially for real-provider smoke and browser behavior. Treat the provider seam not only as a compatibility boundary but also as an authority boundary: document which capabilities and side effects sit behind it today and what later isolation work would need to preserve or reduce.

title: Implement candidate-port smoke-gated deploys with automatic rollback
description: Replace restart-in-place deploys with a blue-green-lite local workflow: immutable release directories or equivalent, per-release environment isolation, candidate startup on a second localhost port, smoke-gated promotion, and automatic rollback to the last known-good release when post-start or post-switch checks fail. Keep the design single-host and low-complexity: one active writer against the real database, brief interruption acceptable, no active-active cluster theatrics. This bead must explicitly handle SQLite-safe promotion rules, schema/migration compatibility or backup-restore requirements, and candidate-mode suppression of background side effects while validation runs.

title: Harden the systemd runtime and document writable-path boundaries
description: Tighten the deployed service sandbox on the current host instead of jumping straight to containers. Inventory the minimum writable paths, required network/address-family access, secret/env exposure, and privilege assumptions, then apply practical systemd hardening options and document the trust boundaries. This bead should explicitly connect sandboxing choices to the single-user tailnet-only threat model and to dependency/supply-chain blast-radius reduction.

title: Document safe-update architecture, assumptions, and rollback runbooks
description: Update repo docs so future-you can understand and operate the safe-update architecture from the repo alone. Cover current-state architecture, why the repo is systemd-first rather than container-first, candidate promotion/rollback runbooks, dependency-update policy by surface, trust-boundary and authority inventories, and an assumptions log showing what was checked versus what remains provisional. Treat documentation as part of the implementation surface, not cleanup.

title: Review the safe-update architecture after contracts, rollback, and sandboxing land
description: Perform a deliberate architecture review after the assumption audit, contract formalization, candidate rollout path, and runtime hardening work land. Judge whether the resulting system actually supports aggressive updates with low operator pain, whether docs and tests match reality, and whether further moves such as service splitting or rootless containers are justified by measured pain rather than fashion.
notes: Review should explicitly ask whether the work improved only rollback/uptime hygiene or also reduced meaningful blast radius from vulnerable or malicious dependencies. It should also judge whether service splitting or rootless containers are now justified by measured authority mismatches or operational pain.

title: Inventory runtime authorities, secret surfaces, and trusted dependency boundaries
description: Produce a first-class authority inventory for the shipped system: which process can read/write which paths, which network destinations/address families are needed, which secrets/env vars are available at runtime, which subprocess or tool authorities exist, and which external codebases or package sources are trusted by the app. Treat the local Hermes tree, background workers, and deploy tooling as dependency/trust surfaces too. The output should support both systemd hardening and honest supply-chain documentation.

title: Keep security lightweight but modern
description: Parent roadmap issue for minimal hardening appropriate to a single-user tailnet-only prototype, plus lightweight automated security hygiene such as SAST and secret scanning.

title: Research mobile browser suspension and SSE reconnect behavior
description: Investigate how iOS Safari and Android browsers behave when the screen is off, the app is backgrounded, or the network changes. Capture practical implications for EventSource reconnect, polling fallback, and transcript replay in Tailchat.
close_reason: Wrote research note docs/research/2026-04-11-mobile-reconnect-research.md with current-code findings, browser-behavior references, and follow-up recommendations

title: Document local run, /hermes deployment, and secret-safe operations
description: Update repo docs so a future operator can run Hermes Tailchat locally or as a user systemd service, expose it under Tailscale Serve at /hermes, and avoid committing secrets. Include explicit guidance for using EnvironmentFile or local env vars instead of putting HERMES_API_KEY in tracked files. Verification should include local health checks and a tailnet smoke path.
close_reason: README, .env.example, and deployment/secret-safe runbook committed on feature branch feat/hermes-tailchat-roadmap-hardening

title: Test mobile layout across portrait and landscape viewports
description: Add repeatable checks or captured artifacts for representative portrait and landscape mobile layouts so future UI work does not quietly regress phone usability.
close_reason: Added repeatable portrait/landscape mobile layout regression tests in tests/test_traceability_and_mobile_layout.py

title: Implement Hermes escalation intake for clarify, escalation, and lesson mail
description: Teach Hermes/Tailchat to monitor only the high-signal Agent Mail traffic directed at Hermes and to respond with decisions, human escalations, or memory/skill updates.

title: Implement bead-splitting and sidecar-review helper workflow
description: Provide helper commands or library support that consistently create implementation, test, and sidecar review beads with the right dependency relationships and traceability expectations.

title: Mobile layout research: capture current pain points on portrait and landscape
description: Inspect the current UI on realistic phone-sized viewports in portrait and landscape. Document concrete readability and navigation issues so mobile improvements target real pain rather than generic responsive design.
close_reason: Captured portrait/landscape mobile pain points in docs/research/2026-04-11-mobile-reconnect-research.md

title: Inventory runtime authorities, secret surfaces, and trusted dependency boundaries
description: Produce a first-class authority inventory for the shipped system: which process can read/write which paths, which network destinations/address families are needed, which secrets/env vars are available at runtime, which subprocess or tool authorities exist, and which external codebases or package sources are trusted by the app. Treat the local Hermes tree, background workers, and deploy tooling as dependency/trust surfaces too. The output should support both systemd hardening and honest supply-chain documentation.

title: Smoke coverage research: choose the fastest stable test harness
description: Evaluate the fastest stable way to protect the current happy path without overbuilding: pytest + TestClient, an HTTP smoke script, browser-level smoke, or a layered mix. Recommend the minimum approach that catches regressions in /hermes routing and core chat flow while keeping iteration speed high.
close_reason: Wrote docs/research/2026-04-11-smoke-harness-research.md recommending a layered smoke approach: pytest-based in-process smoke first, env-gated real Hermes smoke second

title: Add integration tests for the multi-agent coordination loop
description: Add repeatable tests covering ready-work selection, clarification requests, sidecar review bead creation, and done-state traceability evidence.

title: Review deployment docs and secret-handling instructions against a fresh-host checklist
description: Perform a reviewer pass on the runbook and README from the perspective of future-you on a fresh host: can you start the service, configure Tailscale Serve at /hermes, and avoid leaking secrets without consulting chat history?

title: Review third-party action pinning and workflow supply-chain exposure
description: Audit planned or added GitHub workflows for supply-chain risk: unpinned third-party actions, overbroad permissions, artifact trust, and execution paths triggered by untrusted code. Produce concrete hardening follow-ups.

title: Add SAST and secret-scanning guardrails
description: Add lightweight security automation such as CodeQL, Semgrep, or gitleaks so the repo benefits from modern hygiene without slowing iteration too much. Explicitly include secret-leak detection for tracked files and CI-visible reports.

title: Build deterministic Hermes dreaming toolkit and SSH summary
description: Start small with deterministic local tools that support Hermes dreaming: an hourly activity gate, session export/log capture for later AI analysis, skill/memory usage reporting, and an SSH-friendly summary surface. Keep AI usage optional and data-rich, and make the implementation maintainable as a local overlay before upstreaming.
delete_reason: duplicate of hermes-tailchat-zum created during br create retry

title: Review background-job UX and resumability from a mobile perspective
description: Evaluate the background-job experience specifically for a user who leaves the app, comes back later, and wants to quickly understand what happened. Recommend any missing states, timestamps, or summaries.

title: Implement a pragmatic backup/export workflow
description: Add the lightest useful backup/export path for local transcript state so experimentation feels safe without introducing heavy infrastructure.

title: Add deterministic USER.md and MEMORY.md audit/compression tooling
description: Implement deterministic tooling that audits USER.md and MEMORY.md for boilerplate, redundancy, context tax, and demotion candidates, while optimizing for signal rather than chasing a rigid size target. Separate keep-hot, compress, move-to-skills/context-files/cold-store, and remove recommendations.
design: Implement deterministic audit/compression tooling for USER.md and MEMORY.md that scores signal, boilerplate, redundancy, and demotion candidates without blindly minimizing character count.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tool reads built-in memory files and emits structured recommendations.
- Output separates keep-hot, compress, move, and remove classes.
- Recommendations are explainable from deterministic heuristics.
notes: This bead should explicitly avoid a simplistic quota-maximizing optimizer. The goal is to preserve always-on steering value while moving bulky or weakly relevant content into colder layers.

title: Build Hermes/Codex multi-agent coordination workflow
description: Implement the coordination model defined in docs/design/2026-04-13-hermes-codex-multi-agent-coordination.md so Hermes handles high-level clarification/memory, Codex agents self-select work from br/bv, and Agent Mail carries peer coordination and selective escalation.

title: Research backup and export options for local transcript state
description: Compare pragmatic options for protecting local SQLite-backed transcript and job state: documented sqlite backup procedure, export/import command, periodic snapshot, or simple archive flow. Optimize for low maintenance.

title: Establish CI baseline for fast iteration
description: Add a simple CI pipeline that runs the project’s basic checks on every push or pull request. Keep it lightweight but useful: syntax/import checks, smoke tests, and any fast automated coverage that protects the single-user prototype from obvious breakage.

title: Add PR template and commit traceability conventions
description: Add a pull request template and supporting helper behavior so PRs consistently record bead IDs, tests, security notes, and release impact. Also tighten commit traceability conventions so commits can be mapped back to beads more reliably.
close_reason: Added PR template, traceability policy, traceability report script, and ship-pr body generation that includes bead/test/security sections

title: Design Hermes memory hierarchy and frame-of-mind retrieval
description: Turn the research into a concrete architecture for a Hermes memory hierarchy: hot built-in memory, warm skills/procedural frames, cold searchable storage, and frame-of-mind retrieval policies that choose the smallest useful context to inject or fetch. Make the tradeoffs explicit and align the design with official Hermes primitives where possible.
design: Translate the research into an explicit hierarchy: hot built-in memory, warm skills/procedural frames, cold searchable storage, and frame-of-mind retrieval policies. Prefer official Hermes features as the base layer and fill only the smallest necessary gaps locally.
acceptance_criteria:
- Write or update a design artifact that defines hot/warm/cold layers.
- Include retrieval modes such as coding, operator, reflection, and conversational work.
- Explain what data belongs in each layer and why.
notes: The design bead should optimize for maintainability and low context tax, not just novelty. Treat skills as likely carriers of procedural frames, but keep factual memory and cold episodic storage distinct.
close_reason: Wrote the memory hierarchy and frame-of-mind retrieval design doc aligned to official Hermes primitives.

title: Research the lightest useful CI + dependency + SAST stack
description: Choose a minimal automation stack that fits a fast-moving Python prototype: quick checks in CI, low-noise dependency updates, and security scanning with acceptable maintenance overhead.
close_reason: Wrote docs/research/2026-04-11-ci-update-sast-stack-research.md recommending a staged stack: minimal trusted CI + smoke + gitleaks first, Dependabot and alerts next, CodeQL after that

title: Publish role-specific AGENTS guidance for Hermes and Codex agents
description: Add repo-linked guidance docs/templates for Codex workers, Codex reviewers, and Hermes overseers, and link them from the root repo guidance so each agent type has clear operating rules.
close_reason: Role-specific AGENTS docs and markdown assertions are already present and verified via tests/test_multi_agent_design_artifacts.py

title: Hermes dreaming docs and bead alignment for memory hierarchy
description: Update repo docs, design notes, and bead descriptions so the memory-hierarchy work is self-explanatory: what is already built, what official Hermes primitives exist, what local layering is planned, and how research/design/task/review slices relate to development.
design: Keep docs and bead descriptions in sync with the actual memory-hierarchy plan so future work can proceed from beads and repo docs without needing chat archaeology.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repo docs explain the hierarchy and official-Hermes fit clearly.
- Bead descriptions align with docs.
- The relationship between shipped tooling and planned work is easy to understand from the repo alone.
notes: This bead exists because the architecture is subtle; documentation drift would make the later implementation beads much harder to execute correctly.
close_reason: Aligned repo docs and design artifacts around the dreaming toolkit, official Hermes primitives, and the memory hierarchy/retrieval plan.

title: Harden transient retry handling for Hermes and Codex runs
description: Retry transient provider failures only while runs are still pre-side-effect, reset partial streamed output before safe retries, and add regression coverage so Hermes/Codex runs do not duplicate side effects after work has already started.
close_reason: Implemented transient retry guards, partial-output reset handling, safer error surfacing, and regression coverage for Hermes/Codex retry behavior.
delete_reason: duplicate closed retry-hardening slice; keep hermes-tailchat-g9e as canonical bead

title: Research cache poisoning, artifact trust, and workflow handoff risks
description: Model how GitHub Actions caches, artifacts, workflow_run triggers, and cross-workflow handoffs could be abused in this repo. Recommend simple guardrails that fit a personal single-user project.

title: Sidecar review for untrusted-ingestion toolkit foundation
description: Review bead hermes-tailchat-lqx. Check deterministic ingestion logic, source profiles, git metadata handling, risk hint quality, and whether the docs accurately preserve the low-privilege trust boundary for hostile inputs.
close_reason: Sidecar review passed after fixing multiline git commit body parsing in untrusted ingestion toolkit

title: Harden transient retry handling for Hermes and Codex runs
description: Retry transient provider failures only while runs are still pre-side-effect, reset partial streamed output before safe retries, and add regression coverage so Hermes/Codex runs do not duplicate side effects after work has already started.
close_reason: Implemented transient retry guards, partial-output reset handling, safer error surfacing, and regression coverage for Hermes/Codex retry behavior.

title: Add code scanning and secret scanning with actionable triage
description: Introduce lightweight automated scanning that is useful rather than noisy. Cover both code-security findings and accidental secret leakage, with a documented triage approach suitable for a fast-moving personal repo.

title: Enforce PR, commit, and review-bead traceability fields
description: Add checks or helper behavior that require bead refs, validation evidence, and review-bead linkage for multi-agent slices.

title: Add dependency update policy with trust boundaries
description: Configure dependency automation with review-friendly cadence and explicit trust boundaries. Consider grouped updates, safe automerge boundaries if ever used, and how to avoid blind supply-chain churn.

title: Make reconnect and intermittent mobile access resilient
description: Handle cases where the browser loses focus, the screen turns off, or the network drops temporarily. Add event replay/cursor support and reconnect behavior so users can return to an in-progress conversation without losing context or needing a full refresh.

title: Document GitHub branch protection and mandatory-review policy
description: Write down the desired repository policy: feature branches only, mandatory review before merge, and no direct pushes to main. Include how to configure this safely for a personal repo without accidentally permitting unreviewed changes.
close_reason: Added docs/policies/branch-protection-and-pr-flow.md documenting mandatory-review, protected-main, and trusted-branch-only CI policy

title: Design Hermes/Codex multi-agent coordination workflow
description: Produce the end-to-end design doc, role-specific AGENTS guidance templates, message fixtures, and implementation checklist for the Hermes/Codex/beads/Agent Mail system.
close_reason: Added comprehensive design doc, role-specific AGENTS templates, message fixtures, and design-artifact tests for the Hermes/Codex multi-agent workflow

title: Add automated smoke coverage for core chat and /hermes subpath hosting
description: Create automated tests or smoke scripts covering the critical single-user happy path: app boots, /hermes path works, conversations can be created, and a basic Hermes-backed turn succeeds. This protects the working deployment shape from regressions while iteration stays fast.

title: Review smoke coverage against actual regression history
description: Check whether the smoke suite would have caught the /hermes subpath breakage, runtime-environment mismatch, and any obvious startup regressions already seen in this project. Tighten scope if there are blind spots.

title: Implement message-contract helpers and fixtures for Agent Mail coordination
description: Create reusable helpers/fixtures for clarify, review-request, lesson, blocker, and done messages so agent coordination is machine-checkable and consistent.

title: Inventory runtime authorities, secret surfaces, and trusted dependency boundaries
description: Produce a first-class authority inventory for the shipped system: which process can read/write which paths, which network destinations/address families are needed, which secrets/env vars are available at runtime, which subprocess or tool authorities exist, and which external codebases or package sources are trusted by the app. Treat the local Hermes tree, background workers, and deploy tooling as dependency/trust surfaces too. The output should support both systemd hardening and honest supply-chain documentation.

title: Build untrusted-ingestion toolkit foundation
description: Add an initial deterministic-plus-LLM-safe ingestion toolkit for hostile external and semi-external content. Include source code/scripts, pipeline configs, git metadata, websites, email, Discord, logs, and X as untrusted inputs. Ship a small CLI or library foundation with normalized artifact schema, deterministic reduction steps, versioned pipeline configs, and tests. Keep privileged consumers on sanitized artifacts by default.

title: Sidecar review for Codex sanitizer untrusted-ingestion slice
description: Review the new Codex-based low-privilege sanitizer path for hermes-tailchat-lqx, including sandbox flags, minimal environment allowlist, schema-bound output, and tests. Confirm the path keeps hostile input away from privileged contexts and that the README/design docs describe the trust boundary accurately.

title: Extend traceability helpers for bead-linked commits, PRs, and review beads
description: Make the git/PR workflow enforce or strongly encourage bead IDs, tests run, and sidecar review bead references so multi-agent work is auditable.

title: Research secure GitHub Actions policy for untrusted pull requests
description: Determine the safest workflow configuration so CI runs only in trusted contexts and does not execute attacker-controlled code with secrets or elevated permissions. Include pull_request vs pull_request_target guidance, first-time contributor considerations, and fork safety.
close_reason: Wrote docs/research/2026-04-11-github-actions-untrusted-pr-research.md with recommended GitHub Actions and branch protection policy for fast but safe PR shipping

title: Add minimal access-control hardening for a tailnet-only single-user prototype
description: Define and implement the minimum sensible access-control posture for this app given its actual use case: one user, tailnet-only, fast iteration. Evaluate options like trusting Tailscale Serve headers, a simple local-only gate, or documenting why current assumptions are acceptable for now.

title: Support transcript backup/export for local SQLite state
description: Add a pragmatic way to back up or export the local transcript/job state so experimentation feels safer. Keep it simple and local-first: SQLite backups, export/import commands, or documented backup procedures are all acceptable.

title: Implement least-privilege CI workflow defaults
description: Apply secure GitHub Actions defaults such as minimal permissions, concurrency control where useful, and avoidance of secret exposure in untrusted contexts. Treat this as supply-chain hardening, not just CI convenience.
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml with read-only permissions, pinned official actions, concurrency, and a job guard that skips fork PR code execution

title: Design and implement Hermes/Codex background parallel-work orchestration
description: Research the latest Hermes and Codex docs for background/parallel agent workflows, evaluate candidate coordination architectures for Hermes Tailchat, implement the most promising minimal path, and document the decision and operating model.

title: Improve single-user usability and session resilience
description: Parent roadmap issue for the single-user prototype experience: mobile-friendly UI, reconnect behavior, and background-work reliability over Tailscale.

title: Strengthen background job visibility and resumability
description: Improve the server-side background-work workflow so queued/running/completed jobs are easier to inspect from the UI, survive ordinary reconnects, and feel reliable during long-running tasks. Focus on practical visibility and resumability rather than distributed job infrastructure.

title: Prototype frame-of-mind retrieval index for dream artifacts
description: Prototype a deterministic retrieval index over dream artifacts, logs, context files, and other cold storage that can recommend the smallest useful bundle for a given frame of mind or mode such as coding, operator, reflection, or conversational work. Treat this as the first practical bridge between large storage and selectively loaded context.
design: Prototype a deterministic retrieval index over dream artifacts and other cold storage that can choose the smallest useful bundle for a declared frame of mind or mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- Index covers at least dream artifacts and relevant context files/logs.
- Retrieval recommendations differ by mode/frame (for example coding vs operator vs reflection).
- Output is usable as an input bundle selector for later AI analysis.
notes: This is the first practical bridge between large storage and selective retrieval. Keep it deterministic first; semantic/LLM retrieval can come later if needed.
close_reason: Added a deterministic frame-of-mind retrieval index over dream artifacts, context files, and logs, plus tests and retrieval-index output wiring.

title: Set up X API monitoring and cost tracking for high-value accounts
description: Configure X API access and monitoring infrastructure for swyx and similar high-value accounts. Include xurl-based setup, account watchlist config, poll budgets, rate-limit handling, raw/normalized artifact spooling through the untrusted-ingestion boundary, and explicit cost tracking/forecasting for X API plan usage before scaling beyond manual or low-volume polling.
notes: Implemented X API monitor skeleton: watchlist/billing config, safe xurl wrapper, timeline poller with raw+normalized spooling through untrusted ingestion, usage ledger/forecast CLI, docs, and tests. Verified with pytest and swyx poll without emitting raw post text.

title: Reconnect/resume implementation for intermittent mobile sessions
description: Implement reconnection and transcript-refresh behavior that handles browser backgrounding, screen-off periods, and short network interruptions without forcing the user to lose context.

title: Review memory hierarchy plan for official-Hermes fit and upstreamability
description: Review the memory-hierarchy design, audit tooling, and retrieval-index plan against official Hermes behavior, repo goals, and upstream boundaries. Confirm that local layering stays maintainable, that beads/tests/docs cover the work coherently, and that only well-justified abstractions are considered for upstreaming.
design: Perform a review after research, design, docs, and initial tooling/prototype work to confirm the plan still fits official Hermes behavior and does not create unnecessary upstream-conflict risk.
acceptance_criteria:
- Review evaluates research, design, docs, audit tooling, and retrieval prototype together.
- Review calls out any mismatch with official Hermes behavior.
- Review states which abstractions remain local and which might be upstream candidates.
notes: This review is architectural, not just stylistic. It should judge maintainability, completeness, and upstream boundary discipline.

title: Design AI review attestations using signed tags and checklist IDs
description: Design a future mechanism where AI review results are represented as signed attestations tied to specific checklist versions, agent identities, commits or PRs, and merge/release decisions. The attestation should be traceable and verifiable without requiring a separate human GitHub account for routine operation.

title: Implement local API and /hermes route smoke checks
description: Add a fast automated smoke layer that verifies app boot, local health, conversation creation, and /hermes-prefixed API access. This should be the quickest safety net and must run locally and in CI.
close_reason: Added pytest smoke tests covering app boot, /health, conversation creation, jobs, and /hermes-prefixed API access in tests/test_smoke.py; tests pass locally

title: Implement background-job inspection improvements
description: Improve how background jobs are presented so a single user can quickly see queued, running, complete, and failed work, with practical progress or result visibility after returning to the app.

title: Improve mobile-friendly layout and touch ergonomics
description: Make the UI comfortable on phones: responsive layout, readable transcript spacing, better composer sizing, no accidental horizontal overflow, and practical touch targets. Optimize for single-user daily use from a mobile browser over Tailscale.

title: Plan provenance and release-integrity follow-ups
description: Define what level of provenance, signed tags, release attestation, or artifact integrity checking is actually worthwhile for this repo over time. Capture staged recommendations instead of premature ceremony.

title: Research official Hermes primitives for memory hierarchy
description: Review official Hermes docs for built-in memory, memory providers, skills, context files, context references, cron, and hooks; explicitly identify which parts already solve hot/warm/cold storage problems, which parts can approximate frame-of-mind retrieval, and which gaps still require local layering or eventual upstream work.
design: Use official Hermes documentation as the authoritative source. Compare built-in memory, memory providers, skills, context files/references, cron, hooks, and CLI surfaces to the desired RAM/disk/frame-of-mind model.
acceptance_criteria:
- Produce a repo-local research artifact summarizing official Hermes support.
- Explicitly separate what exists now, what can be approximated with official primitives, and what appears to need local layering or upstream work.
- Record implications for the follow-on design bead.
notes: This research should stay concrete and implementation-facing: cite doc-backed capabilities, note relevant CLI/tool surfaces, and avoid hand-wavy philosophy without mapping it back to Hermes behavior.
close_reason: Wrote docs/research/2026-04-19-hermes-memory-hierarchy-primitives.md mapping official Hermes memory, skills, context, cron, and hooks primitives to a hot/warm/cold memory architecture and identifying the remaining orchestration gaps.

title: Build swyx-to-agent-skills ingestion pipeline
description: Create a deterministic-first pipeline that watches swyx publishing surfaces (X, YouTube, smol.ai, Latent Space podcast website), normalizes and sanitizes untrusted content, extracts reusable agent-practice patterns, drafts candidate Hermes/agent SKILL.md artifacts, and routes them through review/promotion before installation. Treat all external content as hostile; privileged agents consume sanitized artifacts by default.

title: Implement Hermes-backed end-to-end smoke flow
description: Add a higher-value smoke that exercises one real Hermes-backed turn end to end, with secrets supplied only from the environment. Keep it opt-in or environment-gated if needed so it does not make ordinary iteration painful.

title: Pin dependency hashes or lockfile integrity where practical
description: Identify all package managers and dependency surfaces in the repo, then pin hashes, lockfile integrity metadata, or equivalent checksum mechanisms where practical. The update workflow should preserve these protections while still supporting rapid upgrades.

title: Build fake coordination harness for Agent Mail, bead, and git tests
description: Create a deterministic fake harness for Agent Mail threads, bead state, and git/PR evidence so multi-agent helpers can be tested without live services.

title: Research free public repo scanners for supply-chain signals
description: Evaluate free public scanning or reputation sources that can help monitor dependency and supply-chain risk in an open-source repo, such as Socket.dev or similar tools. Recommend a low-friction stack appropriate for a personal project.

title: Formalize message envelope schema and subject taxonomy
description: Define the common envelope fields and required subject taxonomy for multi-agent Agent Mail messages so fixtures, helpers, and Hermes intake share a stable contract.
close_reason: Added canonical multi-agent message contract/spec, expanded JSON fixtures with envelope/taxonomy/body rules, and verified with tests/test_multi_agent_design_artifacts.py

title: Add dependency update automation
description: Configure Dependabot or an equivalent mechanism so dependencies stay current with minimal manual work. Keep the setup small and reviewable, with update grouping or cadence chosen to avoid noisy churn in a prototype repo.
notes: Dependency update automation should remain downstream of the safe-update architecture work: the point is to automate updates once contracts, smoke gates, and rollback discipline exist, not to spray PR churn into an under-instrumented system.

title: Operationalize deployment and engineering hygiene
description: Parent roadmap issue for making the working prototype easier to run, safer to maintain, and less fragile under fast iteration: docs, smoke coverage, CI, updates, and backup/restore.

title: Implement responsive mobile layout for chat and navigation
description: Improve the Tailchat UI so chats, transcript, composer, jobs, and approvals are clearly usable on phone-sized screens. Prioritize readability, orientation changes, and low-friction navigation for a single-user mobile browser workflow.
close_reason: Implemented responsive mobile-oriented CSS in app/static/index.html with stacked narrow-screen layout, improved overflow handling, larger touch targets, and landscape-specific rules

title: Enforce non-trivial change classification and sidecar-review requirements
description: Implement the policy helper that decides when a slice is non-trivial and therefore must create or reference a sidecar review bead before completion.
close_reason: Implemented review_requirements helper, PR template guidance, and regression tests for non-trivial/sidecar-review classification.

title: Record review disposition and closeability evidence for implementation beads
description: Implement helper logic and metadata expectations so review disposition, test evidence, and git artifact evidence determine whether an implementation bead can be treated as done.

title: Build deterministic Hermes dreaming toolkit and SSH summary
description: Build a deterministic local-first Hermes dreaming toolkit: hourly idle-gated collection, skill/memory usage reporting, AI-bundle selection, hot/warm/cold memory-hierarchy groundwork, local Hermes overlay maintenance artifacts, and SSH/MOTD summaries. Keep official-Hermes fit and future upstreamability in view while adding small tools that are useful on their own.
design: Keep the parent feature bead aligned with actual shipped scope: deterministic telemetry first, then retrieval hierarchy groundwork, then review/upstream-fit work. Use local layering over official Hermes primitives before considering core patches.
acceptance_criteria:
- Parent bead accurately describes shipped and planned dreaming work.
- Child beads cover research, design, docs, tooling, retrieval prototype, and review.
- Dependency structure matches the real sequence of work.
notes: This bead is intentionally broader than an hourly collector; it now covers the memory-hierarchy groundwork needed to turn dreaming into an architecture rather than a one-off script bundle.
